exemplar.title: To what extent do Nike's promotional strategies help increase its sales?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do Nike's promotional strategies help increase its sales?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Creative concept (âcreativityâ) is visible throughoutâfrom title to conclusion.
Consistent linkage of Nikeâs promotional activities (e.g., SNKRS, influencer collabs) to creative thinking.
weaknesses: Integration is uneven: large descriptive passages list campaigns without deep causal analysis.
Needs more sustained analytical exploration of how creativity directly drives sales outcomes.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a good understanding of the key concept with clear analytical thinking in places, but integration is not yet sustained throughout. This aligns most closely with the 3-mark band.

strengths: Four supporting documents provided, meeting the 3â5 requirement.
Documents (revenue charts, ad-spend data, academic article) are generally relevant to the research question.
weaknesses: Depth is limited: much material serves as generic background rather than probing evidence.
Inconsistent bibliographic formatting undermines professionalism and clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With the correct number of relevant documents but only moderate depth and issues in citation style, the work fits the 3-mark band for supporting documents.

strengths: Appropriate selection of SWOT and force-field analyses covering internal and external factors.
Each tool is applied directly to Nike, demonstrating adequate relevance to the research question.
weaknesses: Applications remain largely descriptive; causal links to sales are not always substantiated.
Force-field analysis lacks a clear rationale for its 1â5 scoring, reducing transparency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The toolkit is adequately selected and applied, but the relevance and analytical depth fall short of the highest band, placing it in the 3-mark descriptor.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Integration of revenue and cost data into a cost-benefit discussion (e.g., Kaepernick campaign).
Inclusion of a dedicated descriptive statistics section provides a basis for analysis.
weaknesses: Analysis is intermittent and often descriptive rather than evaluative.
Primary data sources are outlined in the introduction but not integrated into the analysis; lacks triangulation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is some analysis of the research question using secondary data, but integration is uneven and lacks depth, matching the 3-mark band.

strengths: The conclusion explicitly restates and answers the research question.
Clear, actionable recommendations link back to the analysis.
weaknesses: Some conclusion claims (e.g., COVID recovery figures) are unreferenced or inconsistent with earlier statistics.
Introduces new data in the conclusion rather than summarizing evidence already presented.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the research question is directly addressed, inconsistencies and unreferenced claims prevent a fully evidence-consistent conclusion, aligning with the 2-mark band.

strengths: Clear title page, table of contents and logical sectioning throughout.
Structure enables easy navigation and a coherent flow of ideas.
weaknesses: Table of contents lacks page numbers for sections.
Introduction does not explicitly outline the methodology rationale to guide reader expectations.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: An appropriate organizational structure is maintained throughout, satisfying the 2-mark descriptor for structure.

strengths: All required presentation elements are present (title page, word count, bibliography, charts).
Charts and text are generally coherent and legible.
weaknesses: Force-field diagram labels and legend lack clarity.
A sentence on page 10 is truncated, reducing professionalism.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite minor clarity issues, all required presentation elements are included, fitting the 2-mark band for presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: A clear title page, table of contents and logical sectioning are present, enabling the reader to follow the argument without difficulty.  This demonstrates an âappropriate structure throughoutâ (2 marks).

Charts of revenue, ad-spend and a lengthy academic article are relevant and provide contextual data, but much of the material is generic background rather than probing evidence for the specific research question.  Depth is therefore sufficient but not wide-ranging, warranting the 3-mark level.

All required elementsâtitle page with word count, contents, coherent text, charts and a full bibliographyâare included.  Therefore the presentation strand earns the full 2 marks for required elements.
Four supporting documents are cited and labelled, meeting the 3-5 requirement.  Thus the strand sits in the 3-mark band for number of documents.

Each tool is applied directly to Nike (e.g., grading forces 1â5, linking SWOT points to revenue), demonstrating adequate relevance.  Yet many insights remain descriptive, and causal links to sales are not always substantiated, matching the 3-mark description of ârelevance not always clear but adequately applied.â

The IA uses revenue, advertising-cost and store-count data reasonably well; however, selection is limited to high-level secondary statistics with little triangulation.  This is âadequateâ rather than âsufficientâ, fitting the 3-mark band.

Analysis moves beyond description in placesâe.g., cost-benefit discussion of the Kaepernick campaignâbut generally stops at identifying positives and negatives.  Integration across sections is intermittent, so the work sits comfortably in the 3-mark band for âsome analysis of the research question.â

The final section explicitly restates and answers the research question, weighing positives and negatives of Nikeâs promotional strategies.  This meets the highest strand descriptor (3 marks) for directly answering the RQ.

The student selects SWOT, force-field analysis and basic descriptive statisticsâan adequate, though limited, toolkit that covers both internal and external factors.  This aligns with the âadequate selectionâ descriptor.

The conclusion references earlier findings, yet several claims (e.g., COVID recovery figures) are unreferenced or inconsistent with the statistics given.  Thus only âsomeâ conclusions align with evidence, justifying the 2-mark band.

Nike is consistently at the centre of every section; SWOT and force-field tables, statistical charts and the conclusion all revolve around the single organisation.  Analytical comments explicitly connect each promotional tactic to Nikeâs sales trajectory, showing strongâthough still occasional rather than fullâintegration across the IA.  This satisfies the 4-mark descriptor.

The student keeps the concept of creativity visible from the title page to the concluding section and repeatedly links promotional activities (e.g., SNKRS, âJust Do Itâ, influencer collaborations) to creative thinking.  However the integration is uneven: large descriptive passages merely list campaigns or revenue figures without analysing how creativity underpins the causal chain to sales.  This demonstrates a good, though not yet sustained, integration â aligning most closely with the 3-mark descriptor.

annotations.comment: The title page clearly presents the research question and includes required elements like word count, session, and date, enhancing readability and compliance.
The concept of creativity is stated prominently but not yet fully integrated analytically; deeper exploration of how creativity causally drives sales would strengthen alignment.
The introduction outlines sources but does not follow through with primary data analysis; clarify how primary sources inform your assessment and integrate their findings.
Consider explicitly stating your methodology rationale and structure at the end of the introduction to guide reader expectations.
Selection of a SWOT analysis is appropriate and covers internal and external factors effectively, demonstrating adequate application of strategic tools.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The SWOT discussion is largely descriptive; link each strength and weakness quantitatively to sales figures or campaign outcomes for deeper analysis.
When identifying opportunities, include specific metric-based evidence (e.g., growth rates in emerging markets) to substantiate claims and deepen analysis.
The force-field analysis applies Lewinâs model but omits a clear rationale for the 1â5 ratings; define scoring criteria to enhance transparency.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The force-field diagramâs labels and legend are unclear; add descriptive titles and explain each driving/restraining force to improve clarity.
The evaluative discussion of Nikeâs promotions reads descriptively; integrate specific data points from earlier sections to substantiate each claim.
Including a dedicated descriptive statistics section is valuable; you may enhance it by calculating percentage changes and visualizing trends graphically.
The narrative lists revenue figures but omits growth rate calculations; include percentage comparisons to highlight the impact of key campaigns.
The conclusion restates findings but introduces unreferenced claims; ensure every summary point ties back explicitly to documented evidence.
The sentence at the bottom of page 10 is truncated and lacks closure; review for completeness to maintain professionalism and coherence.
The recommendations section provides clear, actionable suggestions that align with your analysis, demonstrating effective progression from findings to proposals.
Bibliographic entries show inconsistent formatting (e.g., date placement, URL style); adopt a single citation style for professionalism and clarity.
Your table of contents lacks page numbers for each section; adding them would greatly improve navigation and structure.
